name: Xq28_MECP2_duplication_syndrome
description: Xq28 MECP2 duplication syndrome — recurrent ~0.3-2.3 Mb duplications of distal Xq28 encompassing MECP2 and frequently IRAK1, L1CAM. Mediated by NAHR or FoSTeS between K1-K2 SD blocks and other Xq28 inverted/direct duplicons. Distinct from the int22h/F8 inversion locus (more centromeric). X-linked: severe progressive neurological phenotype in males, mild/variable in females.

architecture.internal_structure: Multiple Xq28 SD blocks (K1, K2 cassettes) of ~30-100 kb at >99% identity; high palindrome density in distal Xq28; unique core contains MECP2 (primary dosage gene; gain causes severe X-linked ID), IRAK1, L1CAM, AVPR2, FLNA, GDI1.

related_diseases: MECP2 duplication syndrome (severe X-linked ID in males)
